name: Liquefied Natural Gas
narrative_angle: LNG highlights Singapore's role as a maritime coordination and routing node within Asian energy systems. Singapore does not produce LNG — it organizes the flows between producers and consumers across the Indo-Pacific.

name: Crude Oil
narrative_angle: Crude oil flows through Singapore via the Strait of Malacca — one of the most strategically critical maritime chokepoints on earth. Singapore's refining capacity and trading infrastructure transform crude movement into regional market stability.

name: Bunkering Fuel
narrative_angle: Singapore is the world's largest bunkering port by volume. Bunkering expresses Singapore's logistical sovereignty by sustaining the continuity of global maritime movement — no ship can cross the region without this infrastructure.

name: Fuel Oil
narrative_angle: Fuel oil sits at the intersection of Singapore's refining output and its maritime services economy. The MOPS price benchmark anchors regional fuel oil pricing across Southeast Asia.

name: Palm Oil
narrative_angle: Palm oil reflects Singapore's commercial and routing relevance within Southeast Asian trade ecosystems. Singapore financial institutions and traders structure palm oil contracts between Indonesian and Malaysian producers and global buyers.

name: Industrial Metals
narrative_angle: Metals matter through routing, port-linked movement, industrial supply systems, and trade-oriented logistics. Singapore serves as a key financing and distribution hub for metals flowing between Australian and Latin American producers and Asian manufacturers.

name: Copper
narrative_angle: Copper flows from Chile, Peru, and Australia through Singapore en route to Chinese manufacturing centers. Singapore's commodity traders and banks structure the financing of these long-haul copper movements.

name: Iron Ore
narrative_angle: Iron ore is one of the world's largest seaborne commodity trades. Singapore's SGX hosts the primary iron ore derivatives market, allowing producers and steelmakers to hedge exposure through Singapore's financial infrastructure.

name: Naphtha
narrative_angle: Naphtha links Singapore's refining infrastructure to the Northeast Asian petrochemical industry. Singapore-based traders structure naphtha flows between Middle Eastern refineries and Korean and Japanese petrochemical crackers.

name: Gasoil / Diesel
narrative_angle: Gasoil and diesel pricing in Asia is anchored to Singapore's MOPS benchmark. Singapore's traders manage the arbitrage between Middle East supply, European demand signals, and Asian consumption centers.

name: Natural Gas (Piped)
narrative_angle: Singapore relies on piped natural gas from Indonesia and Malaysia for domestic power generation. This dependency has shaped Singapore's aggressive push toward LNG import diversification, revealing how supply vulnerability drives coordination strategy.

name: Natural Rubber
narrative_angle: The SICOM rubber benchmark, operated from Singapore, sets prices for regional natural rubber trade. Singapore's position between Malaysian and Thai producers and global tire manufacturers makes it the natural pricing center.

name: Thermal Coal
narrative_angle: Singapore-based traders and banks dominate the financing and trading of Indonesian and Australian thermal coal to Southeast and Northeast Asian power generators. Singapore structured this trade even as it positions for energy transition.

name: Jet Fuel / Aviation Turbine Fuel
narrative_angle: Changi Airport's status as Asia's premier aviation hub creates a significant jet fuel market. Singapore's refining and storage infrastructure ensures reliable aviation fuel supply, reinforcing its role as a critical node in global air transportation systems.

name: Petrochemicals
narrative_angle: Jurong Island concentrates one of the world's largest integrated petrochemical complexes. Singapore transforms crude oil and naphtha feedstocks into high-value chemical products exported across the region — a rare case of Singapore adding production value, not just coordination.

name: Agricultural Commodities
narrative_angle: Singapore's commodity trading houses — Wilmar, Olam, Golden Agri — make it the hidden center of global agricultural commodity trade. These Singapore-headquartered groups structure food supply chains from Africa to Southeast Asia.

name: Liquefied Petroleum Gas
narrative_angle: LPG from the Middle East flows through Singapore en route to Asian cooking and industrial markets. Singapore's port infrastructure and trading firms manage the regional redistribution of LPG to smaller Southeast Asian markets.

name: Gold
narrative_angle: Singapore has positioned itself as Asia's premier gold hub, hosting major vault facilities, refineries, and the Monetary Authority's GST exemption on investment gold. Singapore competes with Hong Kong and Dubai for the title of Asia's gold trading center.

name: Tin
narrative_angle: Singapore's proximity to Indonesian and Malaysian tin producers makes it a natural trading center for Southeast Asian tin. Singapore banks and traders structure the financing of tin exports to global solder and electronics manufacturers.

name: Ammonia
narrative_angle: Ammonia is emerging as a key hydrogen carrier for the energy transition. Singapore is positioning to become Asia's ammonia import hub — receiving green ammonia from Australia, the Middle East, and eventually Morocco — converting it back to hydrogen for power generation. This is Singapore's next coordination move.
